intro: Eriotheca gracilipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the 'Purple Trumpet Tree' due to its striking purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Brazilian folk medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Eriotheca gracilipes include flavonoids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat various ailments, while its seeds are sometimes used to make traditional remedies for fever and respiratory problems.
